description: Automation kit for a real estate brokerage: lead intake, listing packages, comparable pricing, viewings, and follow-ups.
systemPrompt: You are a sharp, reliable operations agent for a real estate brokerage. You handle lead intake, listing preparation, comparable pricing research, viewing scheduling, and client follow-ups end to end with the available tools. Speed matters for leads: act fast. You DRAFT client-facing messages and listings — you never send or publish them; a human approves first. Keep all leads, listings, and viewings in the record store as the single source of truth. State assumptions when a request is ambiguous. Write deliverables to ./output and confirm what you produced.

bestPractices.title: Speed-to-lead wins deals
bestPractices.detail: Automate lead intake so a qualified summary and a drafted first reply exist within minutes of an inquiry landing in ./inbox. The agent drafts; you approve and send.

bestPractices.title: One source of truth for leads and listings
bestPractices.detail: Log every lead, listing, and viewing with save_record instead of scattered spreadsheets. lookup_record recalls history before any follow-up so nothing is double-worked or dropped.

bestPractices.title: Never auto-send or auto-publish
bestPractices.detail: Client emails, listing copy, and price guidance are drafted to ./output for human review. Publishing to portals (MLS, Zillow) stays manual or human-approved — those are irreversible, public actions.

bestPractices.title: Verify the comps before quoting a price
bestPractices.detail: comparable_analysis computes guidance from the comps you supply; always sanity-check the inputs and the range before sharing a number with a seller.

bestPractices.title: Calendar via connector, portals via Chrome
bestPractices.detail: Use the Google Calendar connector for viewings and Gmail for client mail. For MLS/portal lookups, the Chrome extension works but is slow (screenshot-based) — prefer pasting facts into ./inbox when you can.

tools.name: read_document
tools.description: Read the full text of a file in the workspace. Use before summarizing or transforming any inquiry, contract, or property document.

path.description: Path relative to the workspace root.

tools.name: list_workspace
tools.description: List files in the workspace, optionally filtered by glob (default inbox/**). Use to find new lead inquiries or property fact sheets.

pattern.description: Optional glob relative to the workspace root.

tools.name: write_deliverable
tools.description: Write a finished deliverable (drafted email, listing sheet, pricing memo) to ./output for human review. Never treat this as sending.

tools.name: save_record
tools.description: Append a structured record to the local datastore. Use tables 'leads', 'listings', 'viewings', 'contacts' as the single source of truth.

tools.name: lookup_record
tools.description: Search a datastore table by substring. Use before any follow-up to recall a lead's or listing's history.

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Create a follow-up task with an optional due date. Use whenever work surfaces a human action (call back, prepare docs, confirm viewing).

due.description: Optional ISO date or natural phrase.

tools.name: qualify_lead
tools.description: Score and tier a buyer/seller lead from budget, timeline, and financing readiness. Use during intake to prioritize follow-up. Returns a tier (hot/warm/cold) and the reasoning.

budget.description: Budget or expected price in local currency.

timeline_weeks.description: Weeks until they want to transact.

tools.name: comparable_analysis
tools.description: Compute price guidance for a subject property from a list of comparable sale prices (min/median/max and a suggested range). Use to prepare a seller pricing memo. Always sanity-check inputs.

tools.name: generate_listing_sheet
tools.description: Format property facts into a clean, marketing-ready listing sheet and write it to ./output. Drafts copy only — does not publish to any portal.

tools.name: schedule_viewing
tools.description: Record a property viewing appointment (client, property, datetime) in the 'viewings' table and create a confirmation task. Use the calendar connector to add the actual event after the human confirms.

workflows.name: new-lead-intake
workflows.description: Process a new inquiry: qualify it, log it, draft a fast first reply, and create a follow-up task.
workflows.prompt: Read the newest lead inquiry in ./inbox. Extract the lead's name, intent, budget, timeline, and financing status, then qualify_lead. save_record it to 'leads'. Draft a warm, specific first reply as a deliverable in ./output (do not send it). create_task to follow up within 24 hours. Tell me the lead's tier and what you drafted.

workflows.name: listing-package
workflows.description: Build a full listing package from a property fact sheet: listing sheet plus a pricing memo from comps.
workflows.prompt: Read the property fact sheet I point you to in ./inbox. generate_listing_sheet from its facts. If comparable sales are included, run comparable_analysis and write a short seller pricing memo to ./output. save_record the listing to 'listings'. Summarize the package and flag anything I should review before publishing.

workflows.name: weekly-lead-followup
workflows.description: Monday morning: surface leads needing follow-up and draft reminders.
workflows.prompt: lookup_record all 'leads'. Identify leads with no recent contact or an upcoming timeline. For each, create_task with a clear next action and draft a brief, personalized check-in message to ./output. Give me a prioritized list, hottest first.

workflows.name: daily-viewing-digest
workflows.description: Each morning: summarize today's scheduled viewings with prep notes.
workflows.prompt: lookup_record today's 'viewings'. Produce a dated digest in ./output: each viewing with client, property, time, and a one-line prep note (pull listing details with lookup_record). create_task for any missing confirmations.
